title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Proxy Mtls Boundaries
summary: An internal control-plane client uses private roots and optional mTLS through an audited proxy without disabling ordinary certificate validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go proxy mtls boundaries increment in a Go 1.26 net/http transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model forward proxies, CONNECT tunnels, TLS interception, and mutual TLS credentials as separate authority boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Proxy Mtls Boundaries · Theory Proxy Mtls Boundaries
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model connects to api3.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Model forward proxies, CONNECT tunnels, TLS interception, and mutual TLS credentials as separate authority boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
